title: festuca occidentalis var. ingrata
intro: Festuca occidentalis var. ingrata, also known as Western fescue, is a perennial grass species native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The health benefits of Festuca occidentalis var. ingrata include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent, which can help to prevent infections. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes.
